questions.question_text: Is our reported fee revenue of $1.2M accurate?
questions.answer_summary: A 20% fee revenue overstatement means $240K in phantom revenue on the P&L — this is a material misstatement that affects regulatory reporting and investor confidence.

questions.question_text: Why are there duplicate transactions and inconsistent payment method classifications?
questions.answer_summary: Duplicate transactions inflate volume metrics used in merchant pricing negotiations, and inconsistent payment method tagging distorts interchange cost allocation by $100K+.

questions.question_text: Which merchant categories and risk tiers have the most data issues?
questions.answer_summary: If data quality issues concentrate in high-risk merchant categories, our fraud models are trained on dirty data — increasing exposure by an estimated 2-3x.

questions.question_text: Should we fix payment method classification or transaction dedup first?
questions.answer_summary: Payment method classification affects every downstream metric (fee calculations, interchange routing, fraud scoring) — fixing it first has the highest multiplier effect.

questions.question_text: If a regulator asks about our settlement time reporting, can we defend it?
questions.answer_summary: Settlement time discrepancies could trigger regulatory scrutiny under payment processing SLAs — inaccurate reporting here has compliance and licensing implications.
